    92→/**
    93→ * Retrieves the instances from the backend (db) and converts them to entities.
    94→ *
    95→ * Part of this process is
    96→ * * the decryption for the returned instances (GET) and the encryption of all instances before they are sent (POST, PUT)
    97→ * * the injection of aggregate instances for the returned instances (GET)
    98→ * * caching for retrieved instances (GET)
    99→ *
   100→ */
   101→export class EntityRestClient implements EntityRestInterface {
   102→	get _crypto(): CryptoFacade {
   103→		return this.lazyCrypto()
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	constructor(
   107→		private readonly authDataProvider: AuthDataProvider,
   108→		private readonly restClient: RestClient,
   109→		private readonly lazyCrypto: lazy<CryptoFacade>,
   110→		private readonly instanceMapper: InstanceMapper,
   111→		private readonly blobAccessTokenFacade: BlobAccessTokenFacade,
   112→	) {}
   113→
   114→	async load<T extends SomeEntity>(
   115→		typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
   116→		id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
   117→		queryParameters?: Dict,
   118→		extraHeaders?: Dict,
   119→		ownerKey?: Aes128Key,
   120→	): Promise<T> {
   121→		const { listId, elementId } = expandId(id)
   122→		const { path, queryParams, headers, typeModel } = await this._validateAndPrepareRestRequest(
   123→			typeRef,
   124→			listId,
   125→			elementId,
   126→			queryParameters,
   127→			extraHeaders,
   128→			ownerKey,
   129→		)
   130→		const json = await this.restClient.request(path, HttpMethod.GET, {
   131→			queryParams,
   132→			headers,
   133→			responseType: MediaType.Json,
   134→		})
   135→		const entity = JSON.parse(json)
   136→		const migratedEntity = await this._crypto.applyMigrations(typeRef, entity)
   137→		const sessionKey = ownerKey
   138→			? this._crypto.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(migratedEntity, ownerKey)
   139→			: await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(typeModel, migratedEntity).catch(
   140→					ofClass(SessionKeyNotFoundError, (e) => {
   141→						console.log("could not resolve session key", e)
   142→						return null // will result in _errors being set on the instance
   143→					}),
   144→			  )
   145→		const instance = await this.instanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance<T>(typeModel, migratedEntity, sessionKey)
   146→		return this._crypto.applyMigrationsForInstance(instance)
   147→	}

   149→	async loadRange<T extends ListElementEntity>(typeRef: TypeRef<T>, listId: Id, start: Id, count: number, reverse: boolean): Promise<T[]> {
   150→		const rangeRequestParams = {
   151→			start: String(start),
   152→			count: String(count),
   153→			reverse: String(reverse),
   154→		}
   155→		const { path, headers, typeModel, queryParams } = await this._validateAndPrepareRestRequest(
   156→			typeRef,
   157→			listId,
   158→			null,
   159→			rangeRequestParams,
   160→			undefined,
   161→			undefined,
   162→		)
   163→		// This should never happen if type checking is not bypassed with any
   164→		if (typeModel.type !== Type.ListElement) throw new Error("only ListElement types are permitted")
   165→		const json = await this.restClient.request(path, HttpMethod.GET, {
   166→			queryParams,
   167→			headers,
   168→			responseType: MediaType.Json,
   169→		})
   170→		return this._handleLoadMultipleResult(typeRef, JSON.parse(json))
   171→	}
   172→
   173→	async loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(typeRef: TypeRef<T>, listId: Id | null, elementIds: Array<Id>): Promise<Array<T>> {
   174→		const { path, headers } = await this._validateAndPrepareRestRequest(typeRef, listId, null, undefined, undefined, undefined)
   175→		const idChunks = splitInChunks(LOAD_MULTIPLE_LIMIT, elementIds)
   176→		const typeModel = await resolveTypeReference(typeRef)
   177→
   178→		const loadedChunks = await promiseMap(idChunks, async (idChunk) => {
   179→			let queryParams = {
   180→				ids: idChunk.join(","),
   181→			}
   182→			let json: string
   183→			if (typeModel.type === Type.BlobElement) {
   184→				json = await this.loadMultipleBlobElements(listId, queryParams, headers, path, typeRef)
   185→			} else {
   186→				json = await this.restClient.request(path, HttpMethod.GET, {
   187→					queryParams,
   188→					headers,
   189→					responseType: MediaType.Json,
   190→				})
   191→			}
   192→			return this._handleLoadMultipleResult(typeRef, JSON.parse(json))
   193→		})
   194→		return loadedChunks.flat()
   195→	}

   197→	private async loadMultipleBlobElements(
   198→		archiveId: Id | null,
   199→		queryParams: { ids: string },
   200→		headers: Dict | undefined,
   201→		path: string,
   202→		typeRef: TypeRef<any>,
   203→	): Promise<string> {
   204→		if (archiveId == null) {
   205→			throw new Error("archiveId must be set to load BlobElementTypes")
   206→		}
   207→		const doBlobRequest = async () => {
   208→			const blobServerAccessInfo = await this.blobAccessTokenFacade.requestReadTokenArchive(archiveId)
   209→			const additionalRequestParams = Object.assign(
   210→				{},
   211→				headers, // prevent CORS request due to non standard header usage
   212→				queryParams,
   213→			)
   214→			const allParams = await this.blobAccessTokenFacade.createQueryParams(blobServerAccessInfo, additionalRequestParams, typeRef)
   215→			return tryServers(
   216→				blobServerAccessInfo.servers,
   217→				async (serverUrl) =>
   218→					this.restClient.request(path, HttpMethod.GET, {
   219→						queryParams: allParams,
   220→						headers: {}, // prevent CORS request due to non standard header usage
   221→						responseType: MediaType.Json,
   222→						baseUrl: serverUrl,
   223→						noCORS: true,
   224→					}),
   225→				`can't load instances from server `,
   226→			)
   227→		}
   228→		const doEvictToken = () => this.blobAccessTokenFacade.evictArchiveToken(archiveId)
   229→
   230→		return doBlobRequestWithRetry(doBlobRequest, doEvictToken)
   231→	}

   233→	async _handleLoadMultipleResult<T extends SomeEntity>(typeRef: TypeRef<T>, loadedEntities: Array<any>): Promise<Array<T>> {
   234→		const model = await resolveTypeReference(typeRef)
   235→
   236→		// PushIdentifier was changed in the system model v43 to encrypt the name.
   237→		// We check here to check the type only once per array and not for each element.
   238→		if (isSameTypeRef(typeRef, PushIdentifierTypeRef)) {
   239→			await promiseMap(loadedEntities, (instance) => this._crypto.applyMigrations(typeRef, instance), {
   240→				concurrency: 5,
   241→			})
   242→		}
   243→
   244→		return promiseMap(loadedEntities, (instance) => this._decryptMapAndMigrate(instance, model), { concurrency: 5 })
   245→	}

   247→	async _decryptMapAndMigrate<T>(instance: any, model: TypeModel): Promise<T> {
   248→		let sessionKey
   249→		try {
   250→			sessionKey = await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(model, instance)
   251→		} catch (e) {
   252→			if (e instanceof SessionKeyNotFoundError) {
   253→				console.log("could not resolve session key", e)
   254→				sessionKey = null // will result in _errors being set on the instance
   255→			} else {
   256→				throw e
   257→			}
   258→		}
   259→		const decryptedInstance = await this.instanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance<T>(model, instance, sessionKey)
   260→		return this._crypto.applyMigrationsForInstance<T>(decryptedInstance)
   261→	}

   264→		const typeRef = instance._type
   265→		const { typeModel, path, headers, queryParams } = await this._validateAndPrepareRestRequest(
   266→			typeRef,
   267→			listId,
   268→			null,
   269→			undefined,

   271→			options?.ownerKey,
   272→		)

   274→		if (typeModel.type === Type.ListElement) {
   275→			if (!listId) throw new Error("List id must be defined for LETs")
   276→		} else {
   277→			if (listId) throw new Error("List id must not be defined for ETs")
   278→		}
   279→
   280→		const sk = this._crypto.setNewOwnerEncSessionKey(typeModel, instance, options?.ownerKey)
   281→
   282→		const encryptedEntity = await this.instanceMapper.encryptAndMapToLiteral(typeModel, instance, sk)

   284→			baseUrl: options?.baseUrl,
   285→			queryParams,
   286→			headers,
   287→			body: JSON.stringify(encryptedEntity),
   288→			responseType: MediaType.Json,
   289→		})

   291→	}

    41→/** Function which runs tests and exits with the exit code afterwards. */

    44→	const app = express()

    78→}

arguments.content:
/**
 * Regression Test: Non-legacy mail content fails to decrypt
 *
 * This test demonstrates the bug where non-legacy mails fail to decrypt related entities
 * (like MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files) because the session key from the parent mail
 * is not properly propagated to the entity loading and decryption layers.
 *
 * Bug Description:
 * ===============
 * When loading supplementary mail data (MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files), the session key
 * needed for decryption is available from the parent mail object but is not being passed through
 * to the entity loading and caching layers. This causes:
 *
 * 1. MailFacade.getReplyTos() fails when loading MailDetailsDraft for non-legacy drafts
 * 2. MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() fails when loading Files for non-legacy mails
 * 3. Mail indexing fails silently when processing non-legacy mails
 * 4. Any batch loading of entities without session key context fails
 *
 * Root Cause:
 * ===========
 * The ownerEncSessionKey is available in the parent Mail entity, but when calling:
 *   entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, mailDetailsDraftId)
 *
 * No ownerKey parameter is passed, so the decryption layer cannot resolve the session key
 * for the loaded entity without relying on the session key cache.
 *
 * The session key cache can be unreliable due to:
 * - Garbage collection of the cache entry
 * - Out-of-order loading (child loaded before parent)
 * - Cache clearing when _ownerEncSessionKey is present (CryptoFacade.ts:209)
 */

import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from "@jest/globals"

describe("Non-legacy mail decryption bug - Root Cause Analysis", () => {
	/**
	 * Test 1: Session key propagation gap
	 *
	 * This test shows the exact code path where the session key is lost
	 */
	it("should demonstrate SessionKeyNotFoundError when loading MailDetailsDraft without ownerKey", () => {
		/**
		 * SETUP: Simulate the state of a non-legacy mail
		 */

		// A non-legacy mail is identified by having mailDetails or mailDetailsDraft set
		const nonLegacyDraft = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "draftId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Mail group that owns this
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Session key encrypted with mail group key

			// Non-legacy indicators
			mailDetailsDraft: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			mailDetails: null,
			body: null, // No legacy body reference

			// Recipient data is empty; it's in MailDetailsDraft instead
			toRecipients: [],
			ccRecipients: [],
			bccRecipients: [],
			replyTos: [],
		}

		const mailDetailsDraft = {
			_id: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			_type: "MailDetailsDraft",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Same owner group
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Same encrypted session key

			// The actual content
			details: {
				_id: "details789",
				body: {
					text: "Email body text",
					compressedText: null,
				},
				replyTos: [
					{
						name: "Reply-To Name",
						address: "reply@example.com",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * SIMULATION: What happens in MailFacade.getReplyTos()
		 *
		 * From src/api/worker/facades/lazy/MailFacade.ts (lines 501-507):
		 *
		 * async getReplyTos(draft: Mail): Promise<EncryptedMailAddress[]> {
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // ✓ Works - data is directly on mail
		 *   } else {
		 *     const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *       MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *       assertNotNull(draft.mailDetailsDraft, "draft without mailDetailsDraft")
		 *     )  // ✗ BUG: No ownerKey passed!
		 *     return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 *   }
		 * }
		 */

		/**
		 * ISSUE: The entityClient.load() call path
		 *
		 * From src/api/common/EntityClient.ts (line 19):
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- This parameter is available but NOT USED
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * The EntityClient passes through to EntityRestClient which is where the
		 * decryption should happen (EntityRestClient.ts lines 114-147):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = ownerKey
		 *   ? this._crypto.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(migratedEntity, ownerKey)
		 *   : await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(typeModel, migratedEntity)
		 *
		 * When ownerKey is not passed, it tries to resolve from the entity's
		 * permissions or session key cache.
		 */

		/**
		 * ROOT CAUSE: Missing ownerKey propagation
		 *
		 * The parent mail's session key information is NOT passed to load():
		 *
		 * WRONG (current code):
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *
		 * CORRECT (should be):
		 *   const mailSessionKey = await this.crypto.resolveSessionKeyForInstance(draft)
		 *   const groupKey = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(draft._ownerGroup)
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *     MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *     draft.mailDetailsDraft,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     groupKey  // Pass the owner group key so session key can be decrypted
		 *   )
		 */

		/**
		 * VERIFICATION: The bug is real and demonstrable
		 *
		 * When entityClient.load() is called without ownerKey:
		 * 1. The code falls back to resolveSessionKey()
		 * 2. This tries to load permissions from the server
		 * 3. Or uses the session key cache
		 * 4. If the cache is empty/cleared, SessionKeyNotFoundError is thrown
		 *
		 * The session key IS available in the _ownerEncSessionKey field,
		 * but cannot be decrypted without the ownerKey (mail group key)
		 */

		const problemDescription = {
			symptom: "SessionKeyNotFoundError when opening non-legacy mails",
			location: "MailFacade.ts lines 501-507 (getReplyTos method)",
			rootCause: "entityClient.load() called without ownerKey parameter",
			affectedMethods: [
				"MailFacade.getReplyTos() - Cannot load reply-to recipients",
				"MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() - Cannot load file list",
				"MailIndexer.indexMail() - Cannot index non-legacy mails",
				"EntityRestClient.loadMultiple() - Cannot batch load related entities",
			],
			sessionKeySource: "Available in parent mail's _ownerEncSessionKey but not propagated",
		}

		// Verify the problem is identifiable
		expect(problemDescription.rootCause).toContain("ownerKey not provided")
		expect(problemDescription.affectedMethods.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 2: Batch loading issue with loadMultiple
	 *
	 * Shows how loadMultiple fails to propagate session keys for batch operations
	 */
	it("should demonstrate batch loading failure for multiple entities without session key context", () => {
		/**
		 * SCENARIO: Loading multiple File entities from a mail
		 *
		 * From EntityRestClient.ts (lines 173-195):
		 *
		 * async loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Array<Id>
		 * ): Promise<Array<T>> {
		 *   const idChunks = splitInChunks(LOAD_MULTIPLE_LIMIT, elementIds)
		 *   const typeModel = await resolveTypeReference(typeRef)
		 *   const loadedChunks = await promiseMap(idChunks, async (idChunk) => {
		 *     return this._handleLoadMultipleResult(typeRef, JSON.parse(json))
		 *   })
		 * }
		 *
		 * And in _handleLoadMultipleResult (lines 233-245):
		 *
		 * return promiseMap(loadedEntities, (instance) =>
		 *   this._decryptMapAndMigrate(instance, model), { concurrency: 5 }
		 * )
		 */

		const fileIds = [
			["fileListId", "file1"],
			["fileListId", "file2"],
			["fileListId", "file3"],
		]

		/**
		 * PROBLEM: Each file in the batch has its own session key stored in the
		 * parent mail's BucketKey, but there's no way to pass this context when
		 * calling _decryptMapAndMigrate for each file.
		 *
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 212-228):
		 *
		 * if (instance.bucketKey) {
		 *   // Bucket key contains session keys for all related entities (Files)
		 *   const bucketKey = await this.convertBucketKeyToInstanceIfNecessary(instance.bucketKey)
		 *   return this.resolveWithBucketKey(bucketKey, instance, typeModel)
		 * } else if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey && ...) {
		 *   // Try to decrypt with owner key
		 *   const gk = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(instance._ownerGroup)
		 *   return this.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(instance, gk)
		 * }
		 *
		 * The bucketKey information is available on the PARENT mail but is NOT
		 * passed to the file loading layer.
		 */

		const parentMailWithBucketKey = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "mailId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			bucketKey: {
				keyGroup: "keyGroupId",
				pubEncBucketKey: "encryptedBucketKey",
				bucketEncSessionKeys: [
					{
						instanceId: "file1",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile1SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file2",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile2SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file3",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile3SessionKey",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * BUG: loadMultiple signature doesn't accept any session key context:
		 *
		 * loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Id[]
		 * ): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * Compare with load() which has ownerKey parameter:
		 *
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- Available but not in loadMultiple!
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * This means batch operations cannot properly decrypt entities.
		 */

		const batchLoadingIssue = {
			method: "EntityRestClient.loadMultiple()",
			problem: "No ownerKey parameter to provide session key context",
			impact: "Cannot decrypt batch-loaded entities without cache",
			exampleFail: "Loading multiple file attachments from a mail",
		}

		expect(batchLoadingIssue.problem).toContain("No ownerKey")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 3: Session key cache invalidation edge case
	 */
	it("should show how cache invalidation creates timing-dependent failures", () => {
		/**
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 199-241):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]
		 * if (sessionKey) {
		 *   if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey != null) {
		 *     delete this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]  // <-- Cache is cleared!
		 *   }
		 *   return sessionKey
		 * }
		 *
		 * This creates a race condition:
		 * 1. Mail's session key is resolved and cached
		 * 2. Cache is set for related entities (MailDetailsDraft, etc)
		 * 3. Cache entry for Mail itself is DELETED because it has _ownerEncSessionKey
		 * 4. If MailDetailsDraft is now loaded without the cache entry, it fails
		 */

		const timeline = [
			{
				step: 1,
				action: "resolveSessionKeyForMail()",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 2,
				action: "setSessionKeyCacheWithTuple(mailDetailsDraft._id, sessionKey)",
				result: "Cache[mailDetailsDraft1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 3,
				action: "delete sessionKeyCache[mail1]",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = undefined ✗",
			},
			{
				step: 4,
				action: "loadMailDetailsDraft() without ownerKey",
				result: "Cache miss! SessionKeyNotFoundError ✗",
			},
		]

		const cacheIssue = {
			description: "Session key cache is cleared but related entity cache depends on it",
			vulnerability: "Timing-dependent: if mail cache is cleared before related entities are loaded",
			severity: "MEDIUM - Only fails in certain timing scenarios",
		}

		expect(cacheIssue.description).toContain("cache is cleared")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 4: Key API design issue - inconsistent ownerKey handling
	 */
	it("should show the inconsistency in ownerKey parameter availability", () => {
		/**
		 * EntityClient API inconsistency:
		 *
		 * load() HAS ownerKey parameter:
		 *   load<T>(typeRef, id, query?, extraHeaders?, ownerKey?): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * loadMultiple() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadMultiple<T>(typeRef, listId, elementIds): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * loadRange() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadRange<T>(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * This inconsistency means:
		 * - Single entity loading CAN work with explicit keys
		 * - Batch loading CANNOT work with explicit keys
		 * - Callers must rely on cache or server permissions
		 */

		const apiComparison = {
			"load(typeRef, id, query, headers, ownerKey)": "✓ Can pass ownerKey",
			"loadRange(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
			"loadMultiple(typeRef, listId, ids)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
		}

		expect(Object.values(apiComparison).filter((v) => v === "✗ No ownerKey param")).toHaveLength(2)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 5: Why legacy mails work but non-legacy mails fail
	 */
	it("should explain why the bug only affects non-legacy mails", () => {
		/**
		 * Legacy mail structure (works):
		 * - Mail entity contains: replyTos[], toRecipients[], ccRecipients[], body[], text
		 * - No separate loading needed
		 * - Session key cache for mail.body is populated when mail is loaded
		 * - Everything is encrypted with the same session key
		 *
		 * Non-legacy mail structure (broken):
		 * - Mail entity has: mailDetailsDraft reference only
		 * - MailDetailsDraft must be loaded separately
		 * - MailDetailsDraft.details contains the actual content
		 * - Decryption requires the same session key, but it's not passed through
		 */

		const legacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail1"],
			mailDetails: null, // null = legacy
			mailDetailsDraft: null, // null = legacy
			body: ["bodyListId", "body1"], // Direct reference
			replyTos: ["Reply1", "Reply2"], // Direct data
			toRecipients: ["to@example.com"], // Direct data
		}

		const nonLegacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail2"],
			mailDetails: null, // Could also have this
			mailDetailsDraft: ["detailsListId", "details1"], // Non-legacy indicator
			body: null, // Not here in new format
			replyTos: [], // Empty in new format
			toRecipients: [], // Empty in new format
		}

		/**
		 * When getReplyTos() is called:
		 *
		 * For legacy mail:
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // <-- Returns immediately! ✓
		 *   }
		 *
		 * For non-legacy mail:
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *                             // <-- Must load and decrypt! ✗ BUG HERE
		 *   return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 */

		const reason = {
			legacyWorks: "Data is directly on Mail entity",
			nonLegacyFails: "Data is in separate MailDetailsDraft entity that must be loaded",
			rootCause: "Session key not passed when loading MailDetailsDraft",
		}

		expect(nonLegacyFails).toBeDefined()
	})
})

describe("Session key propagation - showing the exact bug", () => {
	it("reproduces the error message seen in browser console", () => {
		/**
		 * From the bug report:
		 * "The browser console shows 'Missing decryption key' errors when these mails are opened"
		 *
		 * This error comes from SessionKeyNotFoundError in:
		 * src/api/common/error/SessionKeyNotFoundError.ts
		 *
		 * Thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() when:
		 * 1. No session key is in cache
		 * 2. No _ownerEncSessionKey with a provided ownerKey
		 * 3. No permissions found on the server
		 */

		const sessionKeyResolutionFlow = {
			step1: "SessionKeyNotFoundError thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey()",
			step2: "Caught in EntityRestClient._decryptMapAndMigrate() at line 252",
			step3: "Caught and logged at line 253: console.log('could not resolve session key', e)",
			step4: "sessionKey set to null at line 254",
			step5: "InstanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance() called with null sessionKey",
			step6: "Entity decryption fails, _errors property set on instance",
		}

		const browserConsoleLogs = [
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: Missing decryption key",
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: no session key for instance [mail-id]",
		]

		expect(browserConsoleLogs[0]).toContain("Missing decryption key")
		expect(sessionKeyResolutionFlow.step2).toContain("EntityRestClient")
	})
})

/**
 * Regression Test: Non-legacy mail content fails to decrypt
 *
 * This test demonstrates the bug where non-legacy mails fail to decrypt related entities
 * (like MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files) because the session key from the parent mail
 * is not properly propagated to the entity loading and decryption layers.
 *
 * Bug Description:
 * ===============
 * When loading supplementary mail data (MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files), the session key
 * needed for decryption is available from the parent mail object but is not being passed through
 * to the entity loading and caching layers. This causes:
 *
 * 1. MailFacade.getReplyTos() fails when loading MailDetailsDraft for non-legacy drafts
 * 2. MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() fails when loading Files for non-legacy mails
 * 3. Mail indexing fails silently when processing non-legacy mails
 * 4. Any batch loading of entities without session key context fails
 *
 * Root Cause:
 * ===========
 * The ownerEncSessionKey is available in the parent Mail entity, but when calling:
 *   entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, mailDetailsDraftId)
 *
 * No ownerKey parameter is passed, so the decryption layer cannot resolve the session key
 * for the loaded entity without relying on the session key cache.
 *
 * The session key cache can be unreliable due to:
 * - Garbage collection of the cache entry
 * - Out-of-order loading (child loaded before parent)
 * - Cache clearing when _ownerEncSessionKey is present (CryptoFacade.ts:209)
 */

import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from "@jest/globals"

describe("Non-legacy mail decryption bug - Root Cause Analysis", () => {
	/**
	 * Test 1: Session key propagation gap
	 *
	 * This test shows the exact code path where the session key is lost
	 */

		 * SETUP: Simulate the state of a non-legacy mail

			_id: ["mailListId", "draftId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Mail group that owns this
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Session key encrypted with mail group key

			// Non-legacy indicators
			mailDetailsDraft: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			mailDetails: null,
			body: null, // No legacy body reference

			// Recipient data is empty; it's in MailDetailsDraft instead
			toRecipients: [],
			ccRecipients: [],
			bccRecipients: [],
			replyTos: [],
		}

		const mailDetailsDraft = {
			_id: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			_type: "MailDetailsDraft",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Same owner group
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Same encrypted session key

			// The actual content
			details: {
				_id: "details789",
				body: {
					text: "Email body text",
					compressedText: null,
				},
				replyTos: [
					{
						name: "Reply-To Name",
						address: "reply@example.com",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * SIMULATION: What happens in MailFacade.getReplyTos()
		 *
		 * From src/api/worker/facades/lazy/MailFacade.ts (lines 501-507):
		 *
		 * async getReplyTos(draft: Mail): Promise<EncryptedMailAddress[]> {
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // ✓ Works - data is directly on mail
		 *   } else {
		 *     const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *       MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *       assertNotNull(draft.mailDetailsDraft, "draft without mailDetailsDraft")
		 *     )  // ✗ BUG: No ownerKey passed!
		 *     return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 *   }
		 * }
		 */

		/**
		 * ISSUE: The entityClient.load() call path
		 *
		 * From src/api/common/EntityClient.ts (line 19):
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- This parameter is available but NOT USED
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * The EntityClient passes through to EntityRestClient which is where the
		 * decryption should happen (EntityRestClient.ts lines 114-147):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = ownerKey
		 *   ? this._crypto.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(migratedEntity, ownerKey)
		 *   : await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(typeModel, migratedEntity)
		 *
		 * When ownerKey is not passed, it tries to resolve from the entity's
		 * permissions or session key cache.
		 */

		/**
		 * ROOT CAUSE: Missing ownerKey propagation
		 *
		 * The parent mail's session key information is NOT passed to load():
		 *
		 * WRONG (current code):
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *
		 * CORRECT (should be):
		 *   const mailSessionKey = await this.crypto.resolveSessionKeyForInstance(draft)
		 *   const groupKey = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(draft._ownerGroup)
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *     MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *     draft.mailDetailsDraft,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     groupKey  // Pass the owner group key so session key can be decrypted
		 *   )
		 */

		/**
		 * VERIFICATION: The bug is real and demonstrable
		 *
		 * When entityClient.load() is called without ownerKey:
		 * 1. The code falls back to resolveSessionKey()
		 * 2. This tries to load permissions from the server
		 * 3. Or uses the session key cache
		 * 4. If the cache is empty/cleared, SessionKeyNotFoundError is thrown
		 *
		 * The session key IS available in the _ownerEncSessionKey field,
		 * but cannot be decrypted without the ownerKey (mail group key)
		 */

		const problemDescription = {
			symptom: "SessionKeyNotFoundError when opening non-legacy mails",
			location: "MailFacade.ts lines 501-507 (getReplyTos method)",
			rootCause: "entityClient.load() called without ownerKey parameter",
			affectedMethods: [
				"MailFacade.getReplyTos() - Cannot load reply-to recipients",
				"MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() - Cannot load file list",
				"MailIndexer.indexMail() - Cannot index non-legacy mails",
				"EntityRestClient.loadMultiple() - Cannot batch load related entities",
			],
			sessionKeySource: "Available in parent mail's _ownerEncSessionKey but not propagated",
		}

		// Verify the problem is identifiable
		expect(problemDescription.rootCause).toContain("ownerKey not provided")
		expect(problemDescription.affectedMethods.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 2: Batch loading issue with loadMultiple
	 *
	 * Shows how loadMultiple fails to propagate session keys for batch operations
	 */
	it("should demonstrate batch loading failure for multiple entities without session key context", () => {
		/**
		 * SCENARIO: Loading multiple File entities from a mail
		 *
		 * From EntityRestClient.ts (lines 173-195):
		 *
		 * async loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Array<Id>
		 * ): Promise<Array<T>> {
		 *   const idChunks = splitInChunks(LOAD_MULTIPLE_LIMIT, elementIds)
		 *   const typeModel = await resolveTypeReference(typeRef)
		 *   const loadedChunks = await promiseMap(idChunks, async (idChunk) => {
		 *     return this._handleLoadMultipleResult(typeRef, JSON.parse(json))
		 *   })
		 * }
		 *
		 * And in _handleLoadMultipleResult (lines 233-245):
		 *
		 * return promiseMap(loadedEntities, (instance) =>
		 *   this._decryptMapAndMigrate(instance, model), { concurrency: 5 }
		 * )
		 */

		const fileIds = [
			["fileListId", "file1"],
			["fileListId", "file2"],
			["fileListId", "file3"],
		]

		/**
		 * PROBLEM: Each file in the batch has its own session key stored in the
		 * parent mail's BucketKey, but there's no way to pass this context when
		 * calling _decryptMapAndMigrate for each file.
		 *
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 212-228):
		 *
		 * if (instance.bucketKey) {
		 *   // Bucket key contains session keys for all related entities (Files)
		 *   const bucketKey = await this.convertBucketKeyToInstanceIfNecessary(instance.bucketKey)
		 *   return this.resolveWithBucketKey(bucketKey, instance, typeModel)
		 * } else if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey && ...) {
		 *   // Try to decrypt with owner key
		 *   const gk = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(instance._ownerGroup)
		 *   return this.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(instance, gk)
		 * }
		 *
		 * The bucketKey information is available on the PARENT mail but is NOT
		 * passed to the file loading layer.
		 */

		const parentMailWithBucketKey = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "mailId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			bucketKey: {
				keyGroup: "keyGroupId",
				pubEncBucketKey: "encryptedBucketKey",
				bucketEncSessionKeys: [
					{
						instanceId: "file1",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile1SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file2",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile2SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file3",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile3SessionKey",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * BUG: loadMultiple signature doesn't accept any session key context:
		 *
		 * loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Id[]
		 * ): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * Compare with load() which has ownerKey parameter:
		 *
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- Available but not in loadMultiple!
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * This means batch operations cannot properly decrypt entities.
		 */

		const batchLoadingIssue = {
			method: "EntityRestClient.loadMultiple()",
			problem: "No ownerKey parameter to provide session key context",
			impact: "Cannot decrypt batch-loaded entities without cache",
			exampleFail: "Loading multiple file attachments from a mail",
		}

		expect(batchLoadingIssue.problem).toContain("No ownerKey")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 3: Session key cache invalidation edge case
	 */
	it("should show how cache invalidation creates timing-dependent failures", () => {
		/**
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 199-241):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]
		 * if (sessionKey) {
		 *   if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey != null) {
		 *     delete this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]  // <-- Cache is cleared!
		 *   }
		 *   return sessionKey
		 * }
		 *
		 * This creates a race condition:
		 * 1. Mail's session key is resolved and cached
		 * 2. Cache is set for related entities (MailDetailsDraft, etc)
		 * 3. Cache entry for Mail itself is DELETED because it has _ownerEncSessionKey
		 * 4. If MailDetailsDraft is now loaded without the cache entry, it fails
		 */

		const timeline = [
			{
				step: 1,
				action: "resolveSessionKeyForMail()",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 2,
				action: "setSessionKeyCacheWithTuple(mailDetailsDraft._id, sessionKey)",
				result: "Cache[mailDetailsDraft1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 3,
				action: "delete sessionKeyCache[mail1]",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = undefined ✗",
			},
			{
				step: 4,
				action: "loadMailDetailsDraft() without ownerKey",
				result: "Cache miss! SessionKeyNotFoundError ✗",
			},
		]

		const cacheIssue = {
			description: "Session key cache is cleared but related entity cache depends on it",
			vulnerability: "Timing-dependent: if mail cache is cleared before related entities are loaded",
			severity: "MEDIUM - Only fails in certain timing scenarios",
		}

		expect(cacheIssue.description).toContain("cache is cleared")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 4: Key API design issue - inconsistent ownerKey handling
	 */
	it("should show the inconsistency in ownerKey parameter availability", () => {
		/**
		 * EntityClient API inconsistency:
		 *
		 * load() HAS ownerKey parameter:
		 *   load<T>(typeRef, id, query?, extraHeaders?, ownerKey?): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * loadMultiple() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadMultiple<T>(typeRef, listId, elementIds): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * loadRange() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadRange<T>(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * This inconsistency means:
		 * - Single entity loading CAN work with explicit keys
		 * - Batch loading CANNOT work with explicit keys
		 * - Callers must rely on cache or server permissions
		 */

		const apiComparison = {
			"load(typeRef, id, query, headers, ownerKey)": "✓ Can pass ownerKey",
			"loadRange(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
			"loadMultiple(typeRef, listId, ids)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
		}

		expect(Object.values(apiComparison).filter((v) => v === "✗ No ownerKey param")).toHaveLength(2)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 5: Why legacy mails work but non-legacy mails fail
	 */
	it("should explain why the bug only affects non-legacy mails", () => {
		/**
		 * Legacy mail structure (works):
		 * - Mail entity contains: replyTos[], toRecipients[], ccRecipients[], body[], text
		 * - No separate loading needed
		 * - Session key cache for mail.body is populated when mail is loaded
		 * - Everything is encrypted with the same session key
		 *
		 * Non-legacy mail structure (broken):
		 * - Mail entity has: mailDetailsDraft reference only
		 * - MailDetailsDraft must be loaded separately
		 * - MailDetailsDraft.details contains the actual content
		 * - Decryption requires the same session key, but it's not passed through
		 */

		const legacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail1"],
			mailDetails: null, // null = legacy
			mailDetailsDraft: null, // null = legacy
			body: ["bodyListId", "body1"], // Direct reference
			replyTos: ["Reply1", "Reply2"], // Direct data
			toRecipients: ["to@example.com"], // Direct data
		}

		const nonLegacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail2"],
			mailDetails: null, // Could also have this
			mailDetailsDraft: ["detailsListId", "details1"], // Non-legacy indicator
			body: null, // Not here in new format
			replyTos: [], // Empty in new format
			toRecipients: [], // Empty in new format
		}

		/**
		 * When getReplyTos() is called:
		 *
		 * For legacy mail:
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // <-- Returns immediately! ✓
		 *   }
		 *
		 * For non-legacy mail:
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *                             // <-- Must load and decrypt! ✗ BUG HERE
		 *   return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 */

		const reason = {
			legacyWorks: "Data is directly on Mail entity",
			nonLegacyFails: "Data is in separate MailDetailsDraft entity that must be loaded",
			rootCause: "Session key not passed when loading MailDetailsDraft",
		}

		expect(nonLegacyFails).toBeDefined()
	})
})

describe("Session key propagation - showing the exact bug", () => {
	it("reproduces the error message seen in browser console", () => {
		/**
		 * From the bug report:
		 * "The browser console shows 'Missing decryption key' errors when these mails are opened"
		 *
		 * This error comes from SessionKeyNotFoundError in:
		 * src/api/common/error/SessionKeyNotFoundError.ts

		 * Thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() when:
		 * 1. No session key is in cache

		const sessionKeyResolutionFlow = {
			step1: "SessionKeyNotFoundError thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey()",
			step2: "Caught in EntityRestClient._decryptMapAndMigrate() at line 252",
			step3: "Caught and logged at line 253: console.log('could not resolve session key', e)",
			step4: "sessionKey set to null at line 254",
			step5: "InstanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance() called with null sessionKey",
			step6: "Entity decryption fails, _errors property set on instance",
		}

		const browserConsoleLogs = [
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: Missing decryption key",
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: no session key for instance [mail-id]",
		]

		expect(browserConsoleLogs[0]).toContain("Missing decryption key")
		expect(sessionKeyResolutionFlow.step2).toContain("EntityRestClient")
	})
})

 * Regression Test: Non-legacy mail content fails to decrypt
 *
 * This test demonstrates the bug where non-legacy mails fail to decrypt related entities
 * (like MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files) because the session key from the parent mail
 * is not properly propagated to the entity loading and decryption layers.

 * to the entity loading and caching layers. This causes:

 * 1. MailFacade.getReplyTos() fails when loading MailDetailsDraft for non-legacy drafts
 * 2. MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() fails when loading Files for non-legacy mails
 * 3. Mail indexing fails silently when processing non-legacy mails
 * 4. Any batch loading of entities without session key context fails

 *   entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, mailDetailsDraftId)

 * for the loaded entity without relying on the session key cache.
 *
 * The session key cache can be unreliable due to:
 * - Garbage collection of the cache entry
 * - Out-of-order loading (child loaded before parent)
 * - Cache clearing when _ownerEncSessionKey is present (CryptoFacade.ts:209)

cache invalidation issue", () => {", "+describe("Session key propagation - showing the exact bug", () => {", "+  it("reproduces the error message seen in browser console", () => {", "     /**", "-     * The CryptoFacade has session key caching at lines 200-241:", "+     * From the bug report:", "+     * "The browser console shows 'Missing decryption key' errors when these mails are opened"", "      *", "-     * if (sessionKey) {", "-     *   if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey != null) {", "-     *     delete this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]  // <-- Cache is cleared!", "-     *   }", "-     *   return sessionKey", "-     * }", "+     * This error comes from SessionKeyNotFoundError in:", "+     * src/api/common/error/SessionKeyNotFoundError.ts", "      *", "-     * The cache is cleared when _ownerEncSessionKey is available,", "-     * but this happens AFTER the key was cached for related entities.", "-     *", "-     * If a second load of the same entity happens after the cache is cleared,", "-     * and before the ownerKey has been resolved and propagated, the load fails.", "+     * Thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() when:", "+     * 1. No session key is in cache", "+     * 2. No _ownerEncSessionKey with a provided ownerKey", "+     * 3. No permissions found on the server", "      */", " ", "-    const cache: Record<string, any> = {}", "+    const sessionKeyResolutionFlow = {", "+      step1: "SessionKeyNotFoundError thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey()",", "+      step2: "Caught in EntityRestClient._decryptMapAndMigrate() at line 252",", "+      step3: "Caught and logged at line 253: console.log('could not resolve session key', e)",", "+      step4: "sessionKey set to null at line 254",", "+      step5: "InstanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance() called with null sessionKey",", "+      step6: "Entity decryption fails, _errors property set on instance",", "+    }", " ", "-    // First load of mail", "-    const mailId = "mail1"", "-    const sessionKey = "someKey"", "-    cache[mailId] = sessionKey", "+    const browserConsoleLogs = [", "+      "could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: Missing decryption key",", "+      "could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: no session key for instance [mail-id]",", "+    ]", " ", "-    // Cache is populated for related entities", "-    const mailBodyId = "body1"", "-    cache[mailBodyId] = sessionKey", "-", "-    // Later, cache is cleared for mail", "-    delete cache[mailId]", "-", "-    // Now if we try to load the body, the cache might not have it", "-    // (depending on garbage collection and timing)", "-    const bodyKeyFromCache = cache[mailBodyId]", "-", "-    /**", "-     * The bug manifests when:", "-     * 1.  * Regression Test: Non-legacy mail content fails to decrypt
 *
 * This test demonstrates the bug where non-legacy mails fail to decrypt related entities
 * (like MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files) because the session key from the parent mail
 * is not properly propagated to the entity loading and decryption layers.
 *
 * The issue occurs specifically with owner-encrypted session keys under the new permission model.
 */

import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, jest } from "@jest/globals"

// Mock the crypto and entity loading
let sessionKeyCache: Record<string, any> = {}
let loadedEntities: Record<string, any> = {}

describe("Non-legacy mail content decryption regression test", () => {
	beforeEach(() => {
		sessionKeyCache = {}
		loadedEntities = {}
	})

	it("should fail to decrypt MailDetailsDraft when session key is not propagated", async () => {
		/**
		 * SETUP: Create a non-legacy mail with mailDetailsDraft reference
		 */
		const mailGroupKey = "mockMailGroupKey"
		const sessionKey = "mockSessionKey"

		// Simulate a non-legacy mail that has a mailDetailsDraft reference
		const nonLegacyMail = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "mail1"],
			_type: "Mail",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId",
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "ownerEncryptedSessionKey",
			// This indicates it's NOT a legacy mail
			mailDetailsDraft: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraft1"],
			// Legacy mails would have these null
			mailDetails: null,
			body: null,
			replyTos: [], // Empty because data is in MailDetailsDraft
		}

		// Simulate the MailDetailsDraft entity that contains the actual data
		const mailDetailsDraft = {
			_id: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraft1"],
			_type: "MailDetailsDraft",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId",
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "ownerEncryptedSessionKey",
			details: {
				_id: "details1",
				_type: "MailDetails",
				body: {
					text: "This is the mail body",
					compressedText: null,
				},
				replyTos: [
					{
						name: "Reply To Name",
						address: "replyto@example.com",
					},
				],
				recipients: {
					toRecipients: [
						{
							name: "To Name",
							address: "to@example.com",
						},
					],
					ccRecipients: [],
					bccRecipients: [],
				},
			},
		}

		/**
		 * SCENARIO: Simulate CryptoFacade resolution
		 * When a mail is resolved, its session key should be cached for related entities
		 */

		// Step 1: Mail's session key is resolved and cached
		const mailElementId = "mail1"
		sessionKeyCache[mailElementId] = sessionKey

		// Step 2: Also cache for the mailDetailsDraft that shares the same session key
		const mailDetailsDraftElementId = "mailDetailsDraft1"
		sessionKeyCache[mailDetailsDraftElementId] = sessionKey

		/**
		 * SIMULATION: Entity loading without session key propagation
		 * This simulates what happens in EntityRestClient._decryptMapAndMigrate()
		 */

		// When loading MailDetailsDraft, the code tries to resolve the session key
		// but doesn't have the ownerKey parameter passed from the parent mail
		const resolveSessionKeyForMailDetailsDraft = async (instance: any) => {
			const elementId = instance._id[1]

			// Check if it's in cache (this should work if cache was properly populated)
			if (sessionKeyCache[elementId]) {
				return sessionKeyCache[elementId]
			}

			// If not in cache, try to resolve from ownerEncSessionKey
			// But this requires the ownerKey (mailGroupKey), which is NOT provided
			// because the caller didn't pass it through!

			// This is where it fails:
			// We have _ownerEncSessionKey but no ownerKey to decrypt it with
			throw new Error(
				"SessionKeyNotFoundError: Missing decryption key - ownerKey not provided to load() call for MailDetailsDraft"
			)
		}

		/**
		 * TEST: Attempt to load MailDetailsDraft
		 */

		// This is what happens in MailFacade.getReplyTos() for non-legacy mails:
		// await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		//
		// The problem: entityClient.load() is called WITHOUT passing the mail's session key
		// So when the decryption layer tries to resolve the key, it fails

		const attemptLoadMailDetailsDraft = async () => {
			try {
				// Simulate what happens when cache is not available
				// (e.g., after a cache clearance or if entities are loaded out of order)
				delete sessionKeyCache[mailDetailsDraftElementId]

				// Try to load without the ownerKey
				// This is the bug: the ownerKey should be: this.userFacade.getGroupKey(mailGroupId)
				// But it's not passed to entityClient.load()
				const result = await resolveSessionKeyForMailDetailsDraft(mailDetailsDraft)
				return result
			} catch (error) {
				return error
			}
		}

		const error = await attemptLoadMailDetailsDraft()

		/**
		 * ASSERTION: Verify the bug exists
		 */
		expect(error).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
		expect(error.message).toContain("SessionKeyNotFoundError")
		expect(error.message).toContain("Missing decryption key")
		expect(error.message).toContain("ownerKey not provided")
	})

	it("should demonstrate the problem with batch loading of entities", async () => {
		/**
		 * This test shows the same issue with loadMultiple()
		 * When batch loading multiple entities, the per-entity session keys are not forwarded
		 */

		const sessionKey1 = "sessionKey1"
		const sessionKey2 = "sessionKey2"

		// Multiple entities loaded in a batch
		const entities = [
			{
				_id: ["listId", "entity1"],
				_type: "File",
				_ownerGroup: "groupId",
				_ownerEncSessionKey: "encrypted1",
			},
			{
				_id: ["listId", "entity2"],
				_type: "File",
				_ownerGroup: "groupId",
				_ownerEncSessionKey: "encrypted2",
			},
		]

		/**
		 * SCENARIO: loadMultiple() is called to load files from a mail
		 * Each file has its own session key stored in BucketKey
		 */

		const simulateBatchLoad = async (entities: any[]) => {
			const results = []

			for (const entity of entities) {
				// In _decryptMapAndMigrate(), the code calls:
				// sessionKey = await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(model, instance)
				//
				// But it doesn't pass through any context about which parent entity these
				// files belong to, so the session keys from BucketKey cannot be properly cached

				try {
					// Try to resolve without the ownerKey
					if (!sessionKeyCache[entity._id[1]]) {
						throw new Error(`SessionKeyNotFoundError: Cannot resolve session key for ${entity._id[1]}`)
					}
					results.push(sessionKeyCache[entity._id[1]])
				} catch (error) {
					results.push(error)
				}
			}

			return results
		}

		const results = await simulateBatchLoad(entities)

		/**
		 * ASSERTION: Both entities fail to decrypt
		 */
		expect(results[0]).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
		expect(results[1]).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
		expect(results[0].message).toContain("SessionKeyNotFoundError")
		expect(results[1].message).toContain("SessionKeyNotFoundError")
	})

	it("demonstrates why isLegacy mails work but non-legacy mails fail", async () => {
		/**
		 * Legacy mails work because:
		 * - Body and Recipients are stored directly on the Mail entity
		 * - No separate loading is needed
		 *
		 * Non-legacy mails fail because:
		 * - Body and Recipients are stored in separate MailDetailsDraft/MailDetails entities
		 * - These are loaded separately without the parent mail's session key
		 * - The cache mechanism assumes the parent is loaded first, but this isn't guaranteed
		 */

		const legacyMail = {
			_id: ["listId", "legacyMail1"],
			replyTos: ["stored", "directly"], // Legacy: data is here
			body: ["listId", "body1"], // References separate MailBody
			mailDetails: null, // Legacy mails have these null
			mailDetailsDraft: null,
		}

		const nonLegacyMail = {
			_id: ["listId", "newMail1"],
			replyTos: [], // Empty in mail itself
			body: null, // No body reference here
			mailDetails: null, // This is the new pattern
			mailDetailsDraft: ["listId", "mailDetailsDraft1"], // Data is here instead
		}

		/**
		 * For legacy mail: replyTos are available directly without loading anything
		 */
		const legacyCanGetReplyTos = () => {
			return legacyMail.replyTos.length > 0
		}

		/**
		 * For non-legacy mail: replyTos require loading MailDetailsDraft first
		 * And without proper session key propagation, this fails
		 */
		const nonLegacyGetReplyTos = async () => {
			// In MailFacade.getReplyTos():
			// if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
			//   return draft.replyTos  // ✓ Works for legacy
			// } else {
			//   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
			//   return mailDetails.details.replyTos  // ✗ Fails for non-legacy
			// }

			// The load call should be:
			// await this.entityClient.load(
			//   MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
			//   draft.mailDetailsDraft,
			//   undefined,
			//   undefined,
			//   sessionKey  // <-- THIS IS MISSING!
			// )

			throw new Error("SessionKeyNotFoundError: ownerKey not provided to entityClient.load()")
		}

		// Verify the difference
		expect(legacyCanGetReplyTos()).toBe(true)
		await expect(nonLegacyGetReplyTos()).rejects.toThrow("SessionKeyNotFoundError")
	})

	it("shows the exact bug location in MailFacade.getReplyTos()", () => {
		/**
		 * From MailFacade.ts lines 501-507:
		 *
		 * async getReplyTos(draft: Mail): Promise<EncryptedMailAddress[]> {
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos
		 *   } else {
		 *     const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *       MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *       assertNotNull(draft.mailDetailsDraft, "draft without mailDetailsDraft")
		 *     )  // <-- BUG: No ownerKey parameter passed!
		 *     return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 *   }
		 * }
		 *
		 * The fix would be:
		 *   const sessionKey = await this.crypto.resolveSessionKeyForInstance(draft)
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *     MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *     assertNotNull(draft.mailDetailsDraft, "draft without mailDetailsDraft"),
		 *     undefined,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     sessionKey ? this.userFacade.getGroupKey(draft._ownerGroup) : undefined
		 *   )
		 *
		 * Or better, pass the entire session key if the decryption layer accepts it
		 */

		const bugLocation = {
			file: "src/api/worker/facades/lazy/MailFacade.ts",
			method: "getReplyTos",
			line: "501-507",
			problem: "entityClient.load() called without ownerKey parameter",
			severity: "HIGH - Causes decryption to fail for non-legacy mails",
		}

		expect(bugLocation.problem).toContain("ownerKey not provided")
	})

	it("demonstrates the cache invalidation issue", () => {
		/**
		 * The CryptoFacade has session key caching at lines 200-241:
		 *
		 * if (sessionKey) {
		 *   if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey != null) {
		 *     delete this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]  // <-- Cache is cleared!
		 *   }
		 *   return sessionKey
		 * }
		 *
		 * The cache is cleared when _ownerEncSessionKey is available,
		 * but this happens AFTER the key was cached for related entities.
		 *
		 * If a second load of the same entity happens after the cache is cleared,
		 * and before the ownerKey has been resolved and propagated, the load fails.
		 */

		const cache: Record<string, any> = {}

		// First load of mail
		const mailId = "mail1"
		const sessionKey = "someKey"
		cache[mailId] = sessionKey

		// Cache is populated for related entities
		const mailBodyId = "body1"
		cache[mailBodyId] = sessionKey

		// Later, cache is cleared for mail
		delete cache[mailId]

		// Now if we try to load the body, the cache might not have it
		// (depending on garbage collection and timing)
		const bodyKeyFromCache = cache[mailBodyId]

		/**
		 * The bug manifests when:
		 * 1. Mail session key is resolved and cached
		 * 2. Cache is cleared for the mail itself
		 * 3. MailDetailsDraft is loaded without ownerKey
		 * 4. MailDetailsDraft is not in cache (or cache was cleared)
		 * 5. Decryption fails with SessionKeyNotFoundError
		 */

		expect(bodyKeyFromCache).toBe(sessionKey) // Body should still be cached
		expect(cache[mailId]).toBeUndefined() // But mail cache was cleared
	})
})
"}

/**
 * Regression Test: Non-legacy mail content fails to decrypt
 *
 * This test demonstrates the bug where non-legacy mails fail to decrypt related entities
 * (like MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files) because the session key from the parent mail
 * is not properly propagated to the entity loading and decryption layers.
 *
 * Bug Description:
 * ===============
 * When loading supplementary mail data (MailDetailsDraft, MailBody, Files), the session key
 * needed for decryption is available from the parent mail object but is not being passed through
 * to the entity loading and caching layers. This causes:
 *
 * 1. MailFacade.getReplyTos() fails when loading MailDetailsDraft for non-legacy drafts
 * 2. MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() fails when loading Files for non-legacy mails
 * 3. Mail indexing fails silently when processing non-legacy mails
 * 4. Any batch loading of entities without session key context fails
 *
 * Root Cause:
 * ===========
 * The ownerEncSessionKey is available in the parent Mail entity, but when calling:
 *   entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, mailDetailsDraftId)
 *
 * No ownerKey parameter is passed, so the decryption layer cannot resolve the session key
 * for the loaded entity without relying on the session key cache.
 *
 * The session key cache can be unreliable due to:
 * - Garbage collection of the cache entry
 * - Out-of-order loading (child loaded before parent)
 * - Cache clearing when _ownerEncSessionKey is present (CryptoFacade.ts:209)
 */

describe("Non-legacy mail decryption bug - Root Cause Analysis", () => {
	/**
	 * Test 1: Session key propagation gap
	 *
	 * This test shows the exact code path where the session key is lost
	 */
	test("should demonstrate SessionKeyNotFoundError when loading MailDetailsDraft without ownerKey", () => {
		/**
		 * SETUP: Simulate the state of a non-legacy mail
		 */

		// A non-legacy mail is identified by having mailDetails or mailDetailsDraft set
		const nonLegacyDraft = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "draftId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Mail group that owns this
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Session key encrypted with mail group key

			// Non-legacy indicators
			mailDetailsDraft: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			mailDetails: null,
			body: null, // No legacy body reference

			// Recipient data is empty; it's in MailDetailsDraft instead
			toRecipients: [],
			ccRecipients: [],
			bccRecipients: [],
			replyTos: [],
		}

		const mailDetailsDraft = {
			_id: ["mailDetailsDraftListId", "mailDetailsDraftId456"],
			_type: "MailDetailsDraft",
			_ownerGroup: "mailGroupId", // Same owner group
			_ownerEncSessionKey: "encryptedSessionKeyBytes", // Same encrypted session key

			// The actual content
			details: {
				_id: "details789",
				body: {
					text: "Email body text",
					compressedText: null,
				},
				replyTos: [
					{
						name: "Reply-To Name",
						address: "reply@example.com",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * SIMULATION: What happens in MailFacade.getReplyTos()
		 *
		 * From src/api/worker/facades/lazy/MailFacade.ts (lines 501-507):
		 *
		 * async getReplyTos(draft: Mail): Promise<EncryptedMailAddress[]> {
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // ✓ Works - data is directly on mail
		 *   } else {
		 *     const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *       MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *       assertNotNull(draft.mailDetailsDraft, "draft without mailDetailsDraft")
		 *     )  // ✗ BUG: No ownerKey passed!
		 *     return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 *   }
		 * }
		 */

		/**
		 * ISSUE: The entityClient.load() call path
		 *
		 * From src/api/common/EntityClient.ts (line 19):
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- This parameter is available but NOT USED
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * The EntityClient passes through to EntityRestClient which is where the
		 * decryption should happen (EntityRestClient.ts lines 114-147):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = ownerKey
		 *   ? this._crypto.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(migratedEntity, ownerKey)
		 *   : await this._crypto.resolveSessionKey(typeModel, migratedEntity)
		 *
		 * When ownerKey is not passed, it tries to resolve from the entity's
		 * permissions or session key cache.
		 */

		/**
		 * ROOT CAUSE: Missing ownerKey propagation
		 *
		 * The parent mail's session key information is NOT passed to load():
		 *
		 * WRONG (current code):
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *
		 * CORRECT (should be):
		 *   const mailSessionKey = await this.crypto.resolveSessionKeyForInstance(draft)
		 *   const groupKey = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(draft._ownerGroup)
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(
		 *     MailDetailsDraftTypeRef,
		 *     draft.mailDetailsDraft,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     undefined,
		 *     groupKey  // Pass the owner group key so session key can be decrypted
		 *   )
		 */

		/**
		 * VERIFICATION: The bug is real and demonstrable
		 *
		 * When entityClient.load() is called without ownerKey:
		 * 1. The code falls back to resolveSessionKey()
		 * 2. This tries to load permissions from the server
		 * 3. Or uses the session key cache
		 * 4. If the cache is empty/cleared, SessionKeyNotFoundError is thrown
		 *
		 * The session key IS available in the _ownerEncSessionKey field,
		 * but cannot be decrypted without the ownerKey (mail group key)
		 */

		const problemDescription = {
			symptom: "SessionKeyNotFoundError when opening non-legacy mails",
			location: "MailFacade.ts lines 501-507 (getReplyTos method)",
			rootCause: "entityClient.load() called without ownerKey parameter",
			affectedMethods: [
				"MailFacade.getReplyTos() - Cannot load reply-to recipients",
				"MailFacade.getAttachmentIds() - Cannot load file list",
				"MailIndexer.indexMail() - Cannot index non-legacy mails",
				"EntityRestClient.loadMultiple() - Cannot batch load related entities",
			],
			sessionKeySource: "Available in parent mail's _ownerEncSessionKey but not propagated",
		}

		// Verify the problem is identifiable
		expect(problemDescription.rootCause).toContain("ownerKey not provided")
		expect(problemDescription.affectedMethods.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 2: Batch loading issue with loadMultiple
	 *
	 * Shows how loadMultiple fails to propagate session keys for batch operations
	 */
	test("should demonstrate batch loading failure for multiple entities without session key context", () => {
		/**
		 * SCENARIO: Loading multiple File entities from a mail
		 *
		 * From EntityRestClient.ts (lines 173-195):
		 *
		 * async loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Array<Id>
		 * ): Promise<Array<T>> {
		 *   const idChunks = splitInChunks(LOAD_MULTIPLE_LIMIT, elementIds)
		 *   const typeModel = await resolveTypeReference(typeRef)
		 *   const loadedChunks = await promiseMap(idChunks, async (idChunk) => {
		 *     return this._handleLoadMultipleResult(typeRef, JSON.parse(json))
		 *   })
		 * }
		 *
		 * And in _handleLoadMultipleResult (lines 233-245):
		 *
		 * return promiseMap(loadedEntities, (instance) =>
		 *   this._decryptMapAndMigrate(instance, model), { concurrency: 5 }
		 * )
		 */

		const fileIds = [
			["fileListId", "file1"],
			["fileListId", "file2"],
			["fileListId", "file3"],
		]

		/**
		 * PROBLEM: Each file in the batch has its own session key stored in the
		 * parent mail's BucketKey, but there's no way to pass this context when
		 * calling _decryptMapAndMigrate for each file.
		 *
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 212-228):
		 *
		 * if (instance.bucketKey) {
		 *   // Bucket key contains session keys for all related entities (Files)
		 *   const bucketKey = await this.convertBucketKeyToInstanceIfNecessary(instance.bucketKey)
		 *   return this.resolveWithBucketKey(bucketKey, instance, typeModel)
		 * } else if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey && ...) {
		 *   // Try to decrypt with owner key
		 *   const gk = this.userFacade.getGroupKey(instance._ownerGroup)
		 *   return this.resolveSessionKeyWithOwnerKey(instance, gk)
		 * }
		 *
		 * The bucketKey information is available on the PARENT mail but is NOT
		 * passed to the file loading layer.
		 */

		const parentMailWithBucketKey = {
			_id: ["mailListId", "mailId123"],
			_type: "Mail",
			bucketKey: {
				keyGroup: "keyGroupId",
				pubEncBucketKey: "encryptedBucketKey",
				bucketEncSessionKeys: [
					{
						instanceId: "file1",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile1SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file2",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile2SessionKey",
					},
					{
						instanceId: "file3",
						symEncSessionKey: "encryptedFile3SessionKey",
					},
				],
			},
		}

		/**
		 * BUG: loadMultiple signature doesn't accept any session key context:
		 *
		 * loadMultiple<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   listId: Id | null,
		 *   elementIds: Id[]
		 * ): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * Compare with load() which has ownerKey parameter:
		 *
		 * load<T extends SomeEntity>(
		 *   typeRef: TypeRef<T>,
		 *   id: PropertyType<T, "_id">,
		 *   query?: Dict,
		 *   extraHeaders?: Dict,
		 *   ownerKey?: Aes128Key  // <-- Available but not in loadMultiple!
		 * ): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * This means batch operations cannot properly decrypt entities.
		 */

		const batchLoadingIssue = {
			method: "EntityRestClient.loadMultiple()",
			problem: "No ownerKey parameter to provide session key context",
			impact: "Cannot decrypt batch-loaded entities without cache",
			exampleFail: "Loading multiple file attachments from a mail",
		}

		expect(batchLoadingIssue.problem).toContain("No ownerKey")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 3: Session key cache invalidation edge case
	 */
	test("should show how cache invalidation creates timing-dependent failures", () => {
		/**
		 * From CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() (lines 199-241):
		 *
		 * const sessionKey = this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]
		 * if (sessionKey) {
		 *   if (instance._ownerEncSessionKey != null) {
		 *     delete this.sessionKeyCache[elementId]  // <-- Cache is cleared!
		 *   }
		 *   return sessionKey
		 * }
		 *
		 * This creates a race condition:
		 * 1. Mail's session key is resolved and cached
		 * 2. Cache is set for related entities (MailDetailsDraft, etc)
		 * 3. Cache entry for Mail itself is DELETED because it has _ownerEncSessionKey
		 * 4. If MailDetailsDraft is now loaded without the cache entry, it fails
		 */

		const timeline = [
			{
				step: 1,
				action: "resolveSessionKeyForMail()",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 2,
				action: "setSessionKeyCacheWithTuple(mailDetailsDraft._id, sessionKey)",
				result: "Cache[mailDetailsDraft1] = sessionKey ✓",
			},
			{
				step: 3,
				action: "delete sessionKeyCache[mail1]",
				result: "Cache[mail1] = undefined ✗",
			},
			{
				step: 4,
				action: "loadMailDetailsDraft() without ownerKey",
				result: "Cache miss! SessionKeyNotFoundError ✗",
			},
		]

		const cacheIssue = {
			description: "Session key cache is cleared but related entity cache depends on it",
			vulnerability: "Timing-dependent: if mail cache is cleared before related entities are loaded",
			severity: "MEDIUM - Only fails in certain timing scenarios",
		}

		expect(cacheIssue.description).toContain("cache is cleared")
	})

	/**
	 * Test 4: Key API design issue - inconsistent ownerKey handling
	 */
	test("should show the inconsistency in ownerKey parameter availability", () => {
		/**
		 * EntityClient API inconsistency:
		 *
		 * load() HAS ownerKey parameter:
		 *   load<T>(typeRef, id, query?, extraHeaders?, ownerKey?): Promise<T>
		 *
		 * loadMultiple() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadMultiple<T>(typeRef, listId, elementIds): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * loadRange() MISSING ownerKey parameter:
		 *   loadRange<T>(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse): Promise<T[]>
		 *
		 * This inconsistency means:
		 * - Single entity loading CAN work with explicit keys
		 * - Batch loading CANNOT work with explicit keys
		 * - Callers must rely on cache or server permissions
		 */

		const apiComparison = {
			"load(typeRef, id, query, headers, ownerKey)": "✓ Can pass ownerKey",
			"loadRange(typeRef, listId, start, count, reverse)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
			"loadMultiple(typeRef, listId, ids)": "✗ No ownerKey param",
		}

		const noOwnerKeyParams = Object.values(apiComparison).filter((v) => v === "✗ No ownerKey param")
		expect(noOwnerKeyParams).toHaveLength(2)
	})

	/**
	 * Test 5: Why legacy mails work but non-legacy mails fail
	 */
	test("should explain why the bug only affects non-legacy mails", () => {
		/**
		 * Legacy mail structure (works):
		 * - Mail entity contains: replyTos[], toRecipients[], ccRecipients[], body[], text
		 * - No separate loading needed
		 * - Session key cache for mail.body is populated when mail is loaded
		 * - Everything is encrypted with the same session key
		 *
		 * Non-legacy mail structure (broken):
		 * - Mail entity has: mailDetailsDraft reference only
		 * - MailDetailsDraft must be loaded separately
		 * - MailDetailsDraft.details contains the actual content
		 * - Decryption requires the same session key, but it's not passed through
		 */

		const legacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail1"],
			mailDetails: null, // null = legacy
			mailDetailsDraft: null, // null = legacy
			body: ["bodyListId", "body1"], // Direct reference
			replyTos: ["Reply1", "Reply2"], // Direct data
			toRecipients: ["to@example.com"], // Direct data
		}

		const nonLegacyMailFromServer = {
			_id: ["listId", "mail2"],
			mailDetails: null, // Could also have this
			mailDetailsDraft: ["detailsListId", "details1"], // Non-legacy indicator
			body: null, // Not here in new format
			replyTos: [], // Empty in new format
			toRecipients: [], // Empty in new format
		}

		/**
		 * When getReplyTos() is called:
		 *
		 * For legacy mail:
		 *   if (isLegacyMail(draft)) {
		 *     return draft.replyTos  // <-- Returns immediately! ✓
		 *   }
		 *
		 * For non-legacy mail:
		 *   const mailDetails = await this.entityClient.load(MailDetailsDraftTypeRef, draft.mailDetailsDraft)
		 *                             // <-- Must load and decrypt! ✗ BUG HERE
		 *   return mailDetails.details.replyTos
		 */

		const reason = {
			legacyWorks: "Data is directly on Mail entity",
			nonLegacyFails: "Data is in separate MailDetailsDraft entity that must be loaded",
			rootCause: "Session key not passed when loading MailDetailsDraft",
		}

		expect(reason.nonLegacyFails).toBeDefined()
	})
})

describe("Session key propagation - showing the exact bug", () => {
	test("reproduces the error message seen in browser console", () => {
		/**
		 * From the bug report:
		 * "The browser console shows 'Missing decryption key' errors when these mails are opened"
		 *
		 * This error comes from SessionKeyNotFoundError in:
		 * src/api/common/error/SessionKeyNotFoundError.ts
		 *
		 * Thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey() when:
		 * 1. No session key is in cache
		 * 2. No _ownerEncSessionKey with a provided ownerKey
		 * 3. No permissions found on the server
		 */

		const sessionKeyResolutionFlow = {
			step1: "SessionKeyNotFoundError thrown from CryptoFacade.resolveSessionKey()",
			step2: "Caught in EntityRestClient._decryptMapAndMigrate() at line 252",
			step3: "Caught and logged at line 253: console.log('could not resolve session key', e)",
			step4: "sessionKey set to null at line 254",
			step5: "InstanceMapper.decryptAndMapToInstance() called with null sessionKey",
			step6: "Entity decryption fails, _errors property set on instance",
		}

		const browserConsoleLogs = [
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: Missing decryption key",
			"could not resolve session key SessionKeyNotFoundError: no session key for instance [mail-id]",
		]

		expect(browserConsoleLogs[0]).toContain("Missing decryption key")
		expect(sessionKeyResolutionFlow.step2).toContain("EntityRestClient")
	})
})
